# UI Requirements Checklist: Graph UI Polish and Tables Reorganization

**Purpose**: Validate the completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurability of the UI-facing requirements for graph cleanup, tables reorganization, dashboard validation, and commit-date visibility.
**Created**: 2026-07-02
**Feature**: [/Users/alexandr/local_dev/repositories/my_projects/python_project_inspector/specs/006-graph-ui-polish/spec.md](/Users/alexandr/local_dev/repositories/my_projects/python_project_inspector/specs/006-graph-ui-polish/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the requirements explicit about every UI surface where readable edge-type labels must replace raw identifiers, including any remaining non-sidebar edge-type selectors within feature scope? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how users discover the new top-level Tables tab if they previously relied on graph-page tables, beyond assuming the tab itself is sufficient? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-041, Spec §EC-014]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements defined for the unavailable-state behavior when commit metadata is missing, including whether the neutral state should reserve layout space and avoid misleading chronology cues? [Completeness, Spec §FR-036]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for the metrics dashboard behavior when a level change leaves no valid target or no valid metric, including what users should see before the state becomes queryable again? [Completeness, Spec §FR-046, Spec §EC-015]
- [ ] CHK005 Does the spec intentionally exclude the treemap breadcrumb cleanup and file-detail metadata cleanup that appear in planning/tasks artifacts, or are those user-facing requirements missing from the spec? [Gap, Conflict, Plan §Phase 9, Tasks §US5]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "readable English label" defined precisely enough to judge acceptable capitalization, acronym handling, and separator conversion for generated fallbacks? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-040, Spec §EC-005]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "equivalent bounded margin" constrained enough to avoid materially different pan-bound implementations that still claim compliance? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-038, Spec §A-009]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "remain usable" for wide dynamic-column tables quantified with observable criteria such as scrolling, pinned identifiers, truncation policy, or minimum readable layout expectations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-024, Spec §EC-006]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "clear indication that recalculation occurred" specific enough to distinguish acceptable chart-title metadata from insufficiently subtle visual changes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-032, Spec §SC-010]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the boundary between "content-specific table state" and "safe table UI preferences" explicit enough to classify filters, expanded rows, and search text consistently on snapshot changes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-037, Spec §A-007]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do the sidebar-order requirements align consistently between the spec and plan, especially for where filters, display, forces, focus, and zoom/timelapse appear relative to moved controls? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007, Plan §Phase 2]
- [ ] CHK012 Do the requirements consistently describe the Tables page as sharing the same selected snapshot/commit as Report without implying a separate table-specific selection flow anywhere else? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-037, Spec §A-006]
- [ ] CHK013 Are the metrics validation requirements consistent about whether invalid target/metric values are cleared versus automatically replaced, so the expected post-change state is unambiguous? [Consistency, Spec §FR-028, Spec §FR-045, Spec §EC-008, Spec §EC-015]
- [ ] CHK014 Do commit-date requirements consistently mandate browser-local timezone usage and a fixed numeric format without leaving room for locale-dependent rendering elsewhere in the snapshot header? [Consistency, Spec §FR-034, Spec §FR-042, Spec §FR-043, Spec §SC-017]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK015 Can success for graph viewport recovery be objectively verified without implementation inference, including what counts as "visible graph content" after recovery? [Measurability, Spec §FR-014, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-013]
- [ ] CHK016 Are the success criteria for bounded pan measurable enough to distinguish acceptable exploration padding from excessive empty-space movement? [Measurability, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-038, Spec §SC-014]
- [ ] CHK017 Can the success criteria for readable edge labels be evaluated objectively for mixed cases where some labels come from backend/config and others use generated fallback text? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-015]
- [ ] CHK018 Are the success criteria for the Tables migration measurable enough to confirm both removal from Report and full availability in Tables without relying on implementation knowledge? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-005, Spec §SC-006, Spec §SC-016]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK019 Are alternate navigation scenarios covered for users who switch repeatedly between Report, Tables, and Dashboard while the shared snapshot/commit changes? [Coverage, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-037]
- [ ] CHK020 Are exception-flow requirements complete for zero-node and single-node graphs so viewport recovery and pan bounds remain predictable without loops or false recovery? [Coverage, Spec §EC-001, Spec §EC-002, Spec §EC-012, Spec §EC-013]
- [ ] CHK021 Are recovery requirements complete for timelapse playback when the control is used at first, middle, last, and single-snapshot positions, or are some state transitions left implicit? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013, Spec §EC-003, Spec §EC-004]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements complete for snapshot-change recovery on the Tables page when invalid drilldown, selected module, and preserved preferences interact at the same time? [Coverage, Spec §FR-037, Spec §EC-011, Spec §SC-012]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK023 Are accessibility requirements intentionally excluded or missing for the new Tables top-level tab, moved sidebar accordion sections, and commit-date metadata in the snapshot header? [Gap, Non-Functional]
- [ ] CHK024 Are responsiveness and dense-layout requirements intentionally excluded or missing for the snapshot header and wide tables after adding the Tables tab and commit-date display? [Gap, Non-Functional, Spec §FR-024, Spec §FR-035]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK025 Are backend/config dependencies for edge labels, commit timestamps, and valid dashboard option catalogs documented with enough precision to tell whether frontend behavior is blocked or degraded when data is incomplete? [Dependency, Spec §A-005, Spec §A-010, Spec §A-015]
- [ ] CHK026 Are the assumptions about English-only edge labels and browser-local timezone behavior validated against the broader product requirements, or do they risk conflicting with future localization expectations? [Assumption, Spec §A-004, Spec §A-013, Spec §FR-034]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK027 Does the spec clearly separate "graph page", "snapshot page", and "Report tab" terminology so removal/move requirements cannot be misread as affecting the wrong surface? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-041]
- [ ] CHK028 Are plan/tasks additions for detail cleanup, helper files, and test scope traceable back to explicit spec requirements, or should those items be re-scoped before implementation begins? [Conflict, Plan §Phase 9, Tasks §US5]
